perturbation.original_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement or breach thereof, shall be settled by binding arbitration controlled by the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The number of arbitrator(s) shall be one. The seat of arbitration shall be Houston, Texas. Texas law shall also apply to the extent necessary to fill any gaps created by the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the parties. Judgement of the award rendered by the arbitrator(s) may be entered into any court of competent jurisdiction. If any provision of this Arbitration Agreement is held illegal or unenforceable in a arbitration proceeding, such provision shall be severed and shall be inoperative, and the remainder of this Agreement shall remain operative and binding on the Parties. The arbitrator(s) shall have sole kompetenz-kompetenz regarding this Arbitration Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement or breach thereof, shall be settled by binding arbitration. The number of arbitrator(s) shall be one. The seat of arbitration shall be Houston, Texas. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the parties. Judgement of the award rendered by the arbitrator(s) may be entered into any court of competent jurisdiction. If any provision of this Arbitration Agreement is held illegal or unenforceable in a arbitration proceeding, such provision shall be severed and shall be inoperative, and the remainder of this Agreement shall remain operative and binding on the Parties. The arbitrator(s) shall have sole kompetenz-kompetenz regarding this Arbitration Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "controlled by the rules of the American Arbitration Association" and "Texas law shall also apply to the extent necessary to fill any gaps created by the rules of the American Arbitration Association," the agreement fails to specify which rules govern the arbitration process. This omission creates ambiguity and could leave the arbitration without clear procedural guidelines and without a choice of law for the agreement, thus potentially undermining the enforceability of the arbitration agreement and denying the consultant certain procedural protections. The clause about Texas law filling gaps is also removed, potentially contradicting Texas law requiring clear arbitration guidelines.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the Texas Arbitration Act, which implies the need for clear rules and procedures to govern arbitration. Section 171.041 outlines procedures for initiating arbitration and implies that recognized rules (such as AAA) should be in place. By removing the reference to AAA rules and the choice of law clause, the agreement fails to ensure a structured and fair arbitration process as contemplated by Texas law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.023. PROCEEDING TO STAY ARBITRATION. (a) A court may stay an arbitration commenced or threatened on application and a showing that there is not an agreement to arbitrate. (b) If there is a substantial bona fide dispute as to whether an agreement to arbitrate exists, the court shall try the issue promptly and summarily. (c) The court shall stay the arbitration if the court finds for the party moving for the stay. If the court finds for the party opposing the stay, the court shall order the parties to arbitrate. Acts 1983, 68th Leg., p. 4748, ch. 830, eff. Aug. 29, 1983. Redesignated from Vernon's Ann.Civ.St. art. 238-20, Sec. 4 and amended by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 588, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.024. PLACE FOR MAKING APPLICATION. (a) If there is a proceeding pending in a court involving an issue referable to arbitration under an alleged agreement to arbitrate, a party may make an application under this subchapter only in that court. (b) If Subsection (a) does not apply, a party may make an application in any court, subject to Section 171.096 . Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.025. STAY OF RELATED PROCEEDING. (a) The court shall stay a proceeding that involves an issue subject to arbitration if an order for arbitration or an application for that order is made under this subchapter. (b) The stay applies only to the issue subject to arbitration if that issue is severable from the remainder of the proceeding. Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.026. VALIDITY OF UNDERLYING CLAIM. A court may not refuse to order arbitration because: (1) the claim lacks merit or bona fides; or (2) the fault or ground for the claim is not shown. Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. SUBCHAPTER C. ARBITRATION Sec. 171.041. APPOINTMENT OF ARBITRATORS. (a) The method of appointment of arbitrators is as specified in the agreement to arbitrate. (b) The court, on application of a party stating the nature of the issues to be arbitrated and the qualifications of the proposed arbitrators, shall appoint one or more qualified arbitrators if: (1) the agreement to arbitrate does not specify a method of appointment; (2) the agreed method fails or cannot be followed; or (3) an appointed arbitrator fails or is unable to act and a successor has not been appointed. (c) An arbitrator appointed under Subsection (b) has the powers of an arbitrator named in the agreement to arbitrate. Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.042. MAJORITY ACTION BY ARBITRATORS. The powers of the arbitrators are exercised by a majority unless otherwise provided by the agreement to arbitrate or this chapter. Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.043. HEARING CONDUCTED BY ARBITRATORS. (a) Unless otherwise provided by the agreement to arbitrate, all the arbitrators shall conduct the hearing. A majority of the arbitrators may determine a question and render a final award. (b) If, during the course of the hearing, an arbitrator ceases to act, one or more remaining arbitrators appointed to act as neutral arbitrators may hear and determine the controversy. Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 5.01, eff. Sept. 1, 1997. Sec. 171.044. TIME AND PLACE OF HEARING; NOTICE. (a) Unless otherwise provided by the agreement to arbitrate, the arbitrators shall set a time and place for the hearing and notify each party. (b) The notice must be served not later than the fifth day before the hearing either personally or by registered or certified mail with return receipt requested. Appearance at the hearing waives the notice. (c) The court on application may direct the arbitrators to proceed promptly with the hearing and determination of the controversy. Ad
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the specification that the arbitration will be "controlled by the rules of the American Arbitration Association" and the provision that "Texas law shall also apply to the extent necessary to fill any gaps..." This omission is significant. Section 171.041(a) of the Texas Arbitration Act states, "The method of appointment of arbitrators is as specified in the agreement to arbitrate." While this section focuses on arbitrator appointment, the broader implication is that the agreement to arbitrate should specify the rules and procedures. By removing the reference to AAA rules and the choice of law, the modified agreement creates uncertainty about which rules will govern the arbitration process, potentially contradicting the intent of Texas law to provide for a structured arbitration process. The change removes a degree of certainty and control over the arbitration process, rendering the agreement potentially unenforceable due to lack of clarity in the arbitration rules.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas, without reference to its conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "without reference to its conflicts of law principles," the contract creates ambiguity about which state's laws will apply if there is a conflict of laws issue. The conflicts of law principles determine which jurisdiction's laws apply when there are differences between the laws of different states or countries. If a conflict arises, a Texas court will apply its conflict of law rules to determine which state's law should be used to resolve the conflict. Omitting the phrase creates legal uncertainty, which is potentially non-compliant because it deviates from standard contractual practices that ensure clarity and predictability in legal matters.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text does not directly violate a specific statute, but it contradicts standard conflict of laws principles applied in Texas. While parties can generally choose the governing law, removing the clarification 'without reference to its conflicts of law principles' introduces ambiguity. Texas courts typically apply the Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 to determine the validity and scope of choice-of-law provisions. Omitting this clarification opens the door to potential arguments that another jurisdiction's laws should apply based on conflict of laws principles, potentially undermining the parties' intent and creating legal uncertainty. This creates a legal vulnerability because it deviates from established legal practices designed to ensure clarity in contractual agreements.

perturbation.justification: The changed text, by omitting "without reference to its conflicts of law principles," introduces ambiguity regarding which jurisdiction's laws govern in case of a conflict. The Law Explanation supports that Texas courts typically apply the Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 to determine the validity and scope of choice-of-law provisions and omitting this clarification creates legal uncertainty. The omission deviates from established legal practices designed to ensure clarity in contractual agreements, creating a legal vulnerability.
